description: FlatList renders all items by default causing memory spikes on large datasets
do: Set getItemLayout for fixed-height items and use keyExtractor with stable unique IDs
dont: Don't use ScrollView for dynamic lists — FlatList virtualizes offscreen items automatically

description: ScrollView renders all children at once — inappropriate for long dynamic content
do: Use ScrollView for short static content and FlatList or SectionList for dynamic lists
dont: Don't nest ScrollViews in the same scroll direction — it causes scroll conflicts

description: SafeAreaView from React Native only works on iOS and doesn't handle all edge cases
do: Use react-native-safe-area-context for cross-platform safe area inset handling
dont: Don't use the built-in SafeAreaView — it lacks Android support and dynamic insets

description: Platform-specific logic scattered through components creates unmaintainable spaghetti code
do: Use Platform.select for small differences and .ios.tsx/.android.tsx files for major divergence
dont: Don't litter components with Platform.OS === ios ternaries — extract platform logic cleanly

description: TouchableOpacity is legacy — Pressable is the modern composable alternative
do: Use Pressable with style function for press states — it supports hover and focus too
dont: Don't use TouchableOpacity or TouchableHighlight in new code — Pressable replaces them all

description: The default Image component lacks caching and progressive loading on Android
do: Use expo-image or react-native-fast-image for disk caching and placeholder support
dont: Don't load remote images without a caching layer — it re-downloads on every mount

description: Text in React Native doesn't support all CSS text properties and nesting has rules
do: Nest Text components for inline styling like bold spans within paragraphs
dont: Don't place View components inside Text — only Text can be nested inside Text

description: Duplicated stateful logic across components leads to inconsistency and bugs
do: Extract shared logic into custom hooks with clear naming like useDeviceOrientation
dont: Don't duplicate useEffect patterns across components — extract into a named custom hook

description: Inline style objects are recreated on every render causing unnecessary work
do: Define styles with StyleSheet.create outside the component for cached references
dont: Don't pass inline style objects to components — they create new references each render

description: React Native defaults to flexDirection column unlike web CSS which defaults to row
do: Set flexDirection explicitly and use flex: 1 for expanding children in containers
dont: Don't assume web flex defaults — React Native flex works differently from CSS flexbox

description: Fixed pixel dimensions break on different screen densities and device sizes
do: Use useWindowDimensions hook or percentage-based flex layouts for responsive sizing
dont: Don't hardcode pixel widths — use Dimensions or flex ratios for cross-device compatibility

description: Hardcoded colors and spacing throughout the app make theming and updates impossible
do: Create a theme provider with design tokens accessed via useTheme hook
dont: Don't hardcode hex colors or magic numbers — use theme tokens for all visual values

description: Dark mode requires systematic color management not just inverting colors
do: Use useColorScheme hook and define complete light/dark token sets in your theme
dont: Don't invert colors or use opacity to fake dark mode — define explicit dark palette tokens

description: Shadows and elevation work completely differently on iOS and Android
do: Use Platform.select in StyleSheet for shadows (iOS) and elevation (Android)
dont: Don't use box-shadow syntax — iOS uses shadow* properties and Android uses elevation

description: Incorrect navigation setup causes memory leaks and broken deep links
do: Configure NavigationContainer with linking config and type-safe route params
dont: Don't create navigators inside components — define navigation structure statically

description: Deep links that break on app updates frustrate users and damage retention
do: Configure URL scheme and universal links with path mapping in navigation linking config
dont: Don't hardcode screen names in deep link handlers — use the navigation linking config

description: Tab navigation must persist state across tab switches for good UX
do: Use createBottomTabNavigator with lazy initialization and unmountOnBlur false
dont: Don't destroy tab screens on switch — users expect state persistence across tabs

description: Stack navigation handles screen-to-screen transitions and back gesture
do: Use createNativeStackNavigator for native performance over JS-based stack navigator
dont: Don't use createStackNavigator from @react-navigation/stack — native stack is faster

description: Drawer navigation is common for apps with many top-level sections
do: Use createDrawerNavigator with custom drawer content for branding and user info
dont: Don't nest drawer inside tab navigator — drawer should wrap the root navigation

description: Modals presented as screens integrate better with navigation than the Modal component
do: Present modals as screens in stack navigator with presentation modal option
dont: Don't use the Modal component for complex flows — use navigation modal presentation instead

description: Global state libraries with boilerplate like Redux add unnecessary complexity to most apps
do: Use Zustand with selectors to subscribe only to needed state slices avoiding full rerenders
dont: Don't select the entire store object in components — use granular selectors for each value

description: AsyncStorage is slow for frequent reads and has a 6MB limit on Android
do: Use AsyncStorage for infrequent reads like user preferences and onboarding flags
dont: Don't use AsyncStorage for data read on every render or large datasets — it's too slow

description: MMKV is 30x faster than AsyncStorage with synchronous reads and encryption support
do: Use react-native-mmkv for high-frequency reads and Zustand persist middleware
dont: Don't use AsyncStorage when you need synchronous access or sub-millisecond reads

description: App state lost on restart frustrates users and breaks offline-first experiences
do: Persist critical state with Zustand persist middleware and MMKV storage backend
dont: Don't persist derived state or transient UI state — only persist source-of-truth data

description: Putting everything in global state creates tight coupling and debugging nightmares
do: Limit global state to truly shared data — auth user preferences and cross-screen state
dont: Don't put form values modal visibility or screen-local data in global state

description: Hermes dramatically improves startup time and memory usage with ahead-of-time compilation
do: Enable Hermes in react-native.config.js — it's the default engine in React Native 0.70+
dont: Don't disable Hermes unless you have a specific incompatibility — the benefits are substantial

description: Frequent bridge crossings between JS and native cause frame drops
do: Batch bridge calls and prefer JSI-based libraries that bypass the bridge entirely
dont: Don't call native methods in tight loops or animation frames — batch operations instead

description: Unnecessary rerenders are the primary cause of jank in React Native apps
do: Memoize expensive list item components and stabilize callback references with useCallback
dont: Don't wrap every component in memo — profile first and memoize only where it measurably helps

description: Heavy computation during animations causes frame drops and janky transitions
do: Defer expensive work with InteractionManager.runAfterInteractions for smooth animations
dont: Don't run data processing or heavy state updates during screen transitions

description: JS-driven animations run on the JS thread causing frame drops during heavy computation
do: Set useNativeDriver true for transform and opacity animations to run on the UI thread
dont: Don't use native driver for layout properties like width or height — they're not supported

description: Uncached images re-download on every screen visit wasting bandwidth and causing flicker
do: Use expo-image or FastImage with disk and memory cache and prefetch upcoming images
dont: Don't rely on default Image component caching — it's inconsistent across platforms

description: FlatList without tuning renders too many offscreen items consuming memory
do: Set windowSize to 5 and maxToRenderPerBatch to 10 for balanced performance
dont: Don't set initialNumToRender too high — it delays initial list display on slow devices

description: Event listeners and subscriptions not cleaned up accumulate and crash the app
do: Clean up all subscriptions in useEffect cleanup — event listeners timers and observers
dont: Don't add event listeners or subscriptions without corresponding removal on unmount

description: React Native Animated API is limited and runs on the JS thread causing frame drops
do: Use react-native-reanimated 3 with useSharedValue and useAnimatedStyle for UI thread animations
dont: Don't use the legacy Animated API for complex animations — Reanimated is faster and more capable

description: Touch handling in React Native is limited without native gesture recognition
do: Use react-native-gesture-handler with Reanimated for performant gesture-driven animations
dont: Don't use PanResponder for complex gestures — Gesture Handler runs on the native thread

description: Adding or removing items from lists without animation feels jarring and disorienting
do: Use Reanimated layout animations with entering and exiting props for smooth transitions
dont: Don't manually calculate positions for enter/exit animations — use layout animation presets

description: Shared element transitions connect screens visually and improve perceived performance
do: Use react-native-shared-element or React Navigation 7 shared transitions for hero animations
dont: Don't fake shared transitions with opacity crossfades — use actual shared element libraries

description: Linear timing animations feel robotic and unnatural compared to spring physics
do: Use withSpring with appropriate damping and stiffness values for natural motion
dont: Don't use linear or ease-in-out timing for interactive animations — springs feel more natural

description: iOS users expect platform-specific patterns like blur effects and haptic feedback
do: Use @react-native-community/blur and expo-haptics for iOS-native experiences
dont: Don't ignore iOS conventions — users notice when apps feel non-native on their platform

description: Android users expect Material Design patterns like ripple effects and proper elevation
do: Use Android ripple effect on Pressable and proper elevation for shadows
dont: Don't use iOS-style shadows on Android — use elevation property for material design shadows

description: Permission requests without context have low grant rates and frustrate users
do: Request permissions in context with a pre-permission screen explaining why access is needed
dont: Don't request all permissions at app launch — ask just-in-time when the feature is used

description: Push notifications require platform-specific setup and careful channel management
do: Use notifee or expo-notifications with Android channels and iOS categories configured
dont: Don't send all notifications through a single channel — Android users manage channels individually

description: Biometric auth needs fallback options and secure token storage in keychain
do: Use expo-local-authentication with keychain storage for tokens via expo-secure-store
dont: Don't store auth tokens in AsyncStorage — use platform keychain via SecureStore or Keychain

description: Each platform has different deep linking mechanisms that must be configured separately
do: Configure associated domains for iOS and intent filters for Android in native config files
dont: Don't rely only on custom URL schemes — universal and app links are more reliable

description: Icon buttons and images without labels are invisible to screen reader users
do: Add accessibilityLabel to all interactive elements and meaningful images
dont: Don't use technical or implementation-detail labels like btn-1 — use descriptive human text

description: Correct roles tell screen readers how to present and interact with elements
do: Set accessibilityRole to button header link image or adjustable as appropriate
dont: Don't leave default role on interactive elements — screen readers announce them as generic text

description: Screen readers need state information to convey interactive element status
do: Set accessibilityState for disabled selected checked and expanded states
dont: Don't rely on visual-only indicators for state — screen readers can't see color changes

description: Logical focus order ensures keyboard and screen reader navigation makes sense
do: Set accessibilityViewIsModal on modals and manage focus order with accessible prop
dont: Don't let focus escape behind modals or jump to unexpected elements on screen change

description: Automated tools catch only 30% of accessibility issues — manual testing is essential
do: Test every screen with VoiceOver on iOS and TalkBack on Android before release
dont: Don't skip screen reader testing — automated accessibility checks miss navigation flow issues

description: Users who set large text sizes in system settings expect apps to respect that preference
do: Allow font scaling by default and test layouts at 200% text size for overflow issues
dont: Don't set allowFontScaling false globally — it breaks accessibility for users who need larger text
